question_structure.context: A spring oscillator starts vibrating for the first time after the spring is compressed by $x$, and for the second time after the spring is compressed by $2x$.
question_structure.sub_question_1: Find the ratio of the amplitudes of the two oscillations.

sub_question_1.step_1: According to the definition of the amplitude of simple harmonic motion, when the spring is compressed by $x$ and starts to vibrate for the first time, the amplitude is $x$. When the spring is compressed by $2x$ and starts to vibrate for the second time, the amplitude is $2x$. The ratio of the amplitudes of the two vibrations: $\frac{A_{1}}{A_{2}} = \frac{x}{2x} = \frac{1}{2}$

step_1.physical_theorem: Definition of amplitude in simple harmonic motion

result_quantity.name: Ratio of the amplitudes of the two oscillations
